title: Cheilanthes repens
taxonomy: Cheilanthes repens belongs to the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair fern family. It is a type of fern that is native to certain regions. The species Cheilanthes repens is classified as a genus of ferns within the division Pteridophyta. This division encompasses various types of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Cheilanthes repens is further classified within the class Polypodiopsida, which includes the majority of modern ferns.
uses: Cheilanthes repens is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been employed to treat various ailments, including wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The plant's extracts are also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. In traditional medicine, Cheilanthes repens is often used as a topical application to soothe and calm irritated skin. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for human use.

intro: Cheilanthes repens, also known as the creeping lipfern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cheilanthes repens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Cheilanthes repens is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
